clinical_note.chief_complaint: Follow-up for Type 2 Diabetes and Hypertension
clinical_note.hpi: 58-year-old male with history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (5 years) and Hypertension (8 years) presenting for routine follow-up. Patient reports good compliance with medications. Checking blood sugars at home, ranging 110-145 mg/dL fasting. Denies polyuria, polydipsia, or blurred vision. No chest pain, shortness of breath, or lower extremity edema.

physical_exam.cardiovascular: RRR, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops
physical_exam.abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, normoactive bowel sounds
physical_exam.extremities: No edema, pulses 2+ bilaterally, monofilament exam normal

plan: Continue Metformin 1000mg BID
Continue Lisinopril 20mg daily
Continue Atorvastatin 40mg at bedtime
Reinforce diet and exercise counseling
Recheck HbA1c in 3 months
Annual diabetic eye exam - ordered
Follow-up in 3 months

discharge_summary.admission_diagnosis: Acute exacerbation of congestive heart failure

discharge_diagnosis.diagnosis: Congestive heart failure, acute on chronic, systolic
Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response

discharge_summary.hospital_course: 65-year-old female admitted with 3-day history of worsening dyspnea, orthopnea, and lower extremity edema. Initial BNP elevated at 1850 pg/mL. Chest X-ray showed bilateral pleural effusions and pulmonary vascular congestion. EKG revealed atrial fibrillation with RVR at 124 bpm. Patient was started on IV Lasix with good diuretic response, losing 8 lbs of fluid weight. Rate control achieved with Metoprolol. Echocardiogram showed EF of 35%, moderate mitral regurgitation. Cardiology consulted and recommended optimization of GDMT. Patient's symptoms improved significantly with diuresis. Converted to oral medications on day 3 with stable vitals and improved exercise tolerance.

discharge_medications.name: Furosemide
discharge_medications.instructions: Take in morning and early afternoon

discharge_instructions: Daily weight monitoring - call if gain >3 lbs in 1 day or >5 lbs in 1 week
Fluid restriction 1.5-2 liters per day
Low sodium diet (<2g/day)
Activity as tolerated, cardiac rehab referral placed
Follow-up with Cardiology in 1 week
Follow-up with PCP in 2 weeks

clinical_note.chief_complaint: Right knee pain and stiffness, post-operative follow-up
clinical_note.hpi: 51-year-old male status post right total knee arthroplasty 6 weeks ago for severe osteoarthritis. Patient reports progressive improvement in pain and mobility. Currently ambulating with single-point cane. Performing home physical therapy exercises as instructed. Denies fever, chills, wound drainage, or calf pain. Pain level 3/10 at rest, 5/10 with activity.

right_knee.incision: Well-healed, no erythema, no drainage, staples removed
right_knee.pulses: Dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial 2+ bilaterally

imaging.findings: Prosthesis in good position, no evidence of loosening or periprosthetic fracture

assessment.diagnosis: Status post right total knee arthroplasty

plan: Continue outpatient physical therapy 2-3x/week for 6 more weeks
Wean off opioid pain medication over next 2 weeks
Continue aspirin for 2 more weeks, then discontinue
May progress to cane-free ambulation as tolerated
Return to driving in 2 weeks if off narcotics
Follow-up in 6 weeks with X-rays

discharge_summary.hospital_course: 43-year-old female presented to ED with 2-day history of severe right upper quadrant pain radiating to right shoulder, associated with nausea and vomiting after fatty meal. Labs significant for WBC 14.2, elevated LFTs (AST 89, ALT 112, ALP 156). RUQ ultrasound showed multiple gallstones with gallbladder wall thickening (5mm) and pericholecystic fluid, positive sonographic Murphy's sign. HIDA scan showed non-visualization of gallbladder consistent with acute cholecystitis. Patient made NPO, started on IV fluids and Piperacillin-Tazobactam. Underwent laparoscopic cholecystectomy on hospital day 2 without complications. Operative time 45 minutes, minimal blood loss. Tolerated clear liquids on POD1, advanced to regular diet. Pain well controlled with oral medications. Afebrile with normalizing WBC at discharge.
discharge_summary.pathology: Gallbladder with acute and chronic cholecystitis, multiple cholesterol stones, largest 1.2 cm
discharge_instructions: Incision care: Keep clean and dry, may shower after 48 hours
No heavy lifting (>10 lbs) for 2 weeks
Low-fat diet for 1-2 weeks, then resume normal diet as tolerated
Call if fever >101.5F, worsening pain, wound redness/drainage, or jaundice
Return to work in 1-2 weeks depending on job requirements

signature.credentials: MD, FACS, Board Certified General Surgery

clinical_note.hpi: 68-year-old male with 40 pack-year smoking history, COPD GOLD Stage III, presenting for follow-up after recent ED visit for COPD exacerbation 2 weeks ago. Completed Prednisone taper and Azithromycin course. Reports improvement but still experiencing dyspnea on exertion, able to walk one block before needing to rest. Using rescue inhaler 3-4 times daily. Quit smoking 3 years ago. Denies fever, productive cough, or hemoptysis. Home oxygen at 2L NC at rest, increases to 4L with activity.

physical_exam.general: Chronically ill-appearing male, speaking in short sentences
physical_exam.respiratory: Decreased breath sounds bilaterally, prolonged expiratory phase, scattered expiratory wheezes, no crackles, accessory muscle use noted

spirometry.interpretation: Severe obstruction, no significant bronchodilator response

assessment.diagnosis: COPD with acute exacerbation, now resolving

plan: Add Roflumilast 500mcg daily for frequent exacerbation prevention
Continue current inhaler regimen
Increase home oxygen to 3L at rest, 5L with activity based on 6MWT
Pulmonary rehabilitation referral - 12 week program
Annual influenza vaccine - given today
Pneumococcal vaccines up to date
Follow-up in 4 weeks

signature.credentials: MD, Board Certified Pulmonary and Critical Care Medicine

discharge_summary.admission_diagnosis: Acute ischemic stroke, left MCA territory
discharge_summary.hospital_course: 55-year-old female brought to ED by EMS with acute onset right-sided weakness and speech difficulty. Last known well 90 minutes prior. Initial NIHSS 12. CT head negative for hemorrhage. CTA showed left M1 occlusion. Patient received IV tPA within window and underwent mechanical thrombectomy with successful recanalization (TICI 3). Post-procedure NIHSS improved to 4 with residual right facial droop and mild right arm weakness. MRI showed left MCA territory infarct involving left frontal and parietal lobes. Telemetry revealed new atrial fibrillation. Echo showed normal EF without thrombus. Started on anticoagulation with Apixaban. Speech therapy evaluated - mild dysarthria with intact comprehension, diet upgraded to dysphagia soft. PT/OT evaluated - ambulating with supervision, independent with ADLs. Transferred to acute rehab for continued therapy.
discharge_instructions: Continue all medications as prescribed
Dysphagia diet - soft foods, thin liquids
Fall precautions, use assistive device for ambulation
Signs of stroke: FAST - Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call 911
No driving until cleared by neurology

clinical_note.hpi: 38-year-old G2P1 at 28 weeks 3 days gestation by LMP confirmed by first trimester ultrasound. Pregnancy has been uncomplicated. Reports good fetal movement, denies vaginal bleeding, leakage of fluid, or contractions. Mild lower back discomfort. No headaches, visual changes, or epigastric pain. Reports occasional heartburn controlled with Tums.
plan: Rhogam injection given today (28 week dose)
Tdap vaccine given today
Begin kick counts - 10 movements in 2 hours
Continue prenatal vitamins and supplements
Discussed signs of preterm labor and preeclampsia
Schedule growth ultrasound at 32 weeks
Return in 2 weeks for routine prenatal visit
Transition to weekly visits at 36 weeks

signature.credentials: MD, Board Certified Obstetrics and Gynecology

clinical_note.hpi: 75-year-old male with history of localized prostate adenocarcinoma (Gleason 3+4=7, T2aN0M0) treated with external beam radiation therapy completed 6 months ago. Patient reports good urinary function with mild frequency and nocturia x2. No dysuria, hematuria, or incontinence. Bowel function normal, occasional loose stools. No bone pain. Maintaining good performance status.

genitourinary.dre: Prostate small, firm, no nodules, non-tender

assessment.diagnosis: Prostate cancer, status post definitive radiation

plan: PSA declining appropriately - excellent response to treatment
Continue Tamsulosin for urinary symptoms
Repeat PSA in 3 months
Continue surveillance per NCCN guidelines
Discussed excellent prognosis given PSA response
Annual DRE
Follow-up in 3 months

signature.credentials: MD, Board Certified Urology, Fellowship Trained Urologic Oncology

discharge_summary.admission_diagnosis: Major depressive disorder with suicidal ideation
discharge_summary.hospital_course: 33-year-old female voluntarily admitted to inpatient psychiatry following presentation to ED with suicidal ideation with plan (overdose). Patient reported worsening depression over past 2 months following job loss and relationship difficulties. Denied prior suicide attempts. Was previously on Sertraline 100mg which she self-discontinued 3 months ago due to side effects. On admission, PHQ-9 score 24, Columbia Suicide Severity Score indicated moderate risk. Started on Escitalopram 10mg with plan for titration. Participated actively in group therapy and individual counseling. By day 2, patient reported improvement in mood and denied suicidal ideation. Safety plan developed with patient. Family meeting held with sister who will provide support post-discharge. Discharged with no SI/HI, future-oriented, and engaged with outpatient follow-up plan.
discharge_instructions: Take medications as prescribed, do not stop abruptly
Attend all scheduled outpatient appointments
Implement safety plan if experiencing suicidal thoughts
Crisis hotline: 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Go to nearest ED if experiencing thoughts of self-harm
Avoid alcohol and recreational drugs
Maintain regular sleep schedule
Resume exercise and social activities gradually

safety_plan.environment_safety: Lethal means counseling completed, medications to be stored by sister

clinical_note.hpi: 61-year-old male with 8-year history of seropositive rheumatoid arthritis on current regimen of Methotrexate and Adalimumab. Reports recent flare with increased bilateral hand and wrist pain and stiffness over past 3 weeks. Morning stiffness lasting 90 minutes (previously 20 minutes). No new joint swelling. Denies fever, rash, or eye symptoms. Compliant with medications. Received Adalimumab injection 5 days ago.

musculoskeletal.hands: Bilateral MCP and PIP joint tenderness (2-5 bilaterally), mild synovitis MCP 2-3 bilaterally, no deformities
musculoskeletal.wrists: Bilateral tenderness, mild limitation of extension, no synovitis

plan: Disease flare - increase Prednisone to 10mg daily x 2 weeks, then taper to 5mg
Consider adding Hydroxychloroquine 200mg BID if no improvement
Continue Methotrexate 20mg weekly and Adalimumab every 2 weeks
If inadequate response in 4 weeks, will consider switching biologic
Order hand/wrist X-rays to assess for erosive changes
Repeat labs (CBC, CMP, ESR, CRP) in 4 weeks
Physical therapy referral for hand exercises
Follow-up in 4 weeks, sooner if worsening
